sn56.11:0.3: Setting the Wheel of Dhamma in Motion
sn56.11:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Vārāṇasī in the Deer Park at Isipatana.
sn56.11:1.2: There he addressed the group of five monks:
sn56.11:2.1: “There are these two extremes that are not to be indulged in by one who has gone forth.
sn56.11:2.3: That which is devoted to sensual pleasure in connection with sensuality: base, vulgar, common, ignoble, unprofitable; and that which is devoted to self-affliction: painful, ignoble, unprofitable.
sn56.11:2.4: Avoiding both of these extremes, the middle way realized by the Tathāgata—producing vision, producing knowledge—leads to stilling, to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding.
sn56.11:3.1: “And what is the middle way realized by the Tathāgata that—producing vision, producing knowledge—leads to stilling, to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding?
sn56.11:3.3: right view, right resolve, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right concentration.
sn56.11:3.4: This is the middle way realized by the Tathāgata that—producing vision, producing knowledge—leads to stilling, to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding.
sn56.11:4.1: “Now this, monks, is the noble truth of stress:
sn56.11:4.2: Birth is stressful, aging is stressful, death is stressful; sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair are stressful; association with the unbeloved is stressful, separation from the loved is stressful, not getting what is wanted is stressful. In short, the five clinging-aggregates are stressful.
sn56.11:4.3: “And this, monks, is the noble truth of the origination of stress:
sn56.11:4.4: the craving that makes for further becoming—accompanied by passion & delight, relishing now here & now there—i.e.,
sn56.11:4.5: craving for sensuality, craving for becoming, craving for non-becoming.
sn56.11:4.6: “And this, monks, is the noble truth of the cessation of stress:
sn56.11:4.7: the remainderless fading & cessation, renunciation, relinquishment, release, & letting go of that very craving.
sn56.11:4.8: “And this, monks, is the noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of stress:
sn56.11:4.10: right view, right resolve, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right concentration.
sn56.11:5.1: “Vision arose, insight arose, discernment arose, knowledge arose, illumination arose within me with regard to things never heard before: ‘This is the noble truth of stress’ …
sn56.11:5.2: ‘This noble truth of stress is to be comprehended’ …
sn56.11:5.3: ‘This noble truth of stress has been comprehended.’
sn56.11:6.1: “Vision arose, insight arose, discernment arose, knowledge arose, illumination arose within me with regard to things never heard before: ‘This is the noble truth of the origination of stress’ …
sn56.11:6.2: ‘This noble truth of the origination of stress is to be abandoned’5 …
sn56.11:6.3: ‘This noble truth of the origination of stress has been abandoned.’
sn56.11:7.1: “Vision arose, insight arose, discernment arose, knowledge arose, illumination arose within me with regard to things never heard before: ‘This is the noble truth of the cessation of stress’ …
sn56.11:7.2: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of stress is to be realized’ …
sn56.11:7.3: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of stress has been realized.’
sn56.11:8.1: “Vision arose, insight arose, discernment arose, knowledge arose, illumination arose within me with regard to things never heard before: ‘This is the noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of stress’ …
sn56.11:8.2: ‘This noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of stress is to be developed’ …
sn56.11:8.3: ‘This noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of stress has been developed.’6
sn56.11:9.1: “And, monks, as long as this—my three-round, twelve-permutation knowledge & vision concerning these four noble truths7 as they have come to be—was not pure, I did not claim to have directly awakened to the right self-awakening unexcelled in the cosmos with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk.
sn56.11:10.1: But as soon as this—my three-round, twelve-permutation knowledge & vision concerning these four noble truths as they have come to be—was truly pure, then I did claim to have directly awakened to the right self-awakening unexcelled in the cosmos with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk.
sn56.11:10.3: ‘Unprovoked8 is my release. This is the last birth. There is now no further becoming.’”
sn56.11:10.4: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn56.11:10.5: Gratified, the group of five monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
sn56.11:11.1: And while this explanation was being given, there arose to Ven. Kondañña the dustless, stainless Dhamma eye:
sn56.11:11.2: Whatever is subject to origination is all subject to cessation.
sn56.11:12.1: And when the Blessed One had set the Wheel of Dhamma in motion, the earth devas cried out:
sn56.11:12.2: “Near Vārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana, the Blessed One has set in motion the unexcelled Wheel of Dhamma that cannot be stopped by contemplative or brahman, deva, Māra, or Brahmā, or anyone at all in the cosmos.”
sn56.11:12.3: On hearing the earth devas’ cry, the Devas of the Four Great Kings took up the cry…
sn56.11:12.9: the Devas [Muses?] Wielding Power over the Creations of Others…
sn56.11:12.10: the Devas of Brahmā’s Retinue took up the cry:
sn56.11:12.11: “Near Vārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana, the Blessed One has set in motion the unexcelled Wheel of Dhamma that cannot be stopped by contemplative or brahman, deva, Māra, or Brahmā, or anyone at all in the cosmos.”
sn56.11:13.1: So in that moment, that instant, the cry shot right up to the Brahmā worlds.
sn56.11:13.2: And this ten-thousand-fold cosmos shivered & quivered & quaked, while a great, measureless radiance appeared in the cosmos, surpassing the effulgence of the deities.
sn56.11:14.2: “So you really know, Kondañña? So you really know?”
sn56.11:14.3: And that is how Ven. Kondañña acquired the name Añña-Kondañña—Kondañña who knows.
